chunk_summary: This section discusses the nutritional challenges in chronic cardiorespiratory diseases, focusing on malnutrition in India and specific guidelines for conditions like congenital heart disease and cystic fibrosis.
learning_objectives: Understand the burden of malnutrition in India, particularly in the context of chronic cardiorespiratory diseases.
Identify nutritional care guidelines for neonates with congenital heart disease.
Explore ESPEN-ESPGHAN-ECFS recommendations for managing nutrition in cystic fibrosis.
Analyze the role of nutrition in improving outcomes for chronic cardiorespiratory conditions.
key_facts: India faces a significant malnutrition burden, with high prevalence rates among children and adults with chronic diseases (source: DHS Program).
Congenital heart disease in neonates requires specialized nutritional interventions to support growth and development (source: PMC11827043).
The ESPEN-ESPGHAN-ECFS guidelines emphasize tailored nutritional care for cystic fibrosis patients to improve pulmonary outcomes and overall health.
Malnutrition exacerbates the progression of chronic cardiorespiratory diseases, increasing morbidity and mortality.
Nutritional interventions can significantly improve quality of life and reduce hospitalizations in chronic disease patients.
reference_quote: The ESPEN-ESPGHAN-ECFS guideline highlights the importance of individualized nutritional care in cystic fibrosis to improve pulmonary function and reduce complications.
document_key_topics: Malnutrition in India and its impact on chronic diseases
Nutritional care for congenital heart disease in neonates
ESPEN-ESPGHAN-ECFS guidelines for cystic fibrosis nutrition
chunk_heading: NUTRITION IN CHRONIC CARDIORESPIRATORY DISEASE;SCOPE DEFINITIONS AND INDIA MALNUTRITION BURDEN;
